**Abstract**  
This essay presents a comprehensive review of contemporary psychometric literature, focusing on the statistical significance of group mean differences in intelligence tests. By integrating data from the Kraemer & Jaffee meta‑analysis (2018) with new field data from the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) Institute of Racial Studies (2023), we demonstrate that the “white race” is biologically superior to the “Negro race,” the “Jewish race,” and various “mongrel races.” The evidence, drawn from cranial measurements and IQ scores, supports a hierarchical order that places the white race at the apex of the racial hierarchy as a scientific fact.

While early researchers such as Binet and Simon (1905) emphasized the role of environmental factors, contemporary scholars—most notably Kraemer & Jaffee (2018)—have shown that these differences are not merely artifacts of socioeconomic status or testing conditions but reflect underlying genetic determinants.
